prompt_text: Open source or affordable customer operations tools instead of Help Scout
response:
# Open Source & Affordable Help Scout Alternatives

## Open Source Options

| Tool | Cost | Best For |
|------|------|----------|
| **Zulip** | Free/self-hosted | Team chat + support workflows |
| **Chatwoot** | Free/self-hosted | Omnichannel messaging (email, chat, social) |
| **Plane** | Free/self-hosted | Issue tracking + customer projects |
| **OpenSupports** | Free/self-hosted | Basic ticket system |
| **Freescout** | Free/self-hosted | Email helpdesk clone |

## Low-Cost Paid Solutions

| Tool | Price | Highlights |
|------|-------|-----------|
| **Freshdesk** | $15-165/mo | Feature-rich, good value |
| **Zendesk** | $19-199/mo | Industry standard, scalable |
| **Hive** | $15-65/mo | Project + support features |
| **Groove** | $15-99/mo | Simple, modern design |
| **Jira Service Management** | $10-665/mo | Dev-friendly, flexible |

## Best Budget-Friendly Picks

**Chatwoot** (self-hosted) — Best overall value
- Omnichannel support
- Automation & bots
- Free deployment option

**Freshdesk** — Best paid alternative
- Automation & AI features
- Good reporting
- Reasonable per-agent pricing

**Zulip** — Best for internal + customer ops
- Team communication integrated with support

---

**What's your priority?** Omnichannel messaging, automation, team collaboration, or pure cost?
